steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
The sum of all interior angles in a triangle equals 180 degrees. With \angle K = 48 degrees and \angle L = 48 degrees, set up the equation:
$$\angle J + 48^{\circ} + 48^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Solve for angle J
steps.content:
Simplify the left side:
$$\angle J + 96^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$
Subtract 96 degrees from both sides:
$$\angle J = 84^{\circ}$$
The answer is 84.
